summaryForNext: All 4 goals MET (G1 OAuth endpoint auth+rate-limit, G2 bcrypt secrets, G3 identity-classification edges, G4 actor_token reject + Hydra-delegate). QA 4/4 PASS first-pass, zero blocking-constraint violations. Separated security review caught 1 HIGH (delegate reused the redirect-following shared reqwest client -> compromised Hydra 3xx could exfiltrate the raw subject_token; fixed pre-archive with a dedicated redirect(Policy::none()) client + test) plus 3 proactive hardenings. Sycophancy gate: PASS (s08=false, score 0.0). Open debt: (1) delegate mode un-downscoped -> delegated tokens escape flint_kind agent-budget classification (by design, documented); (2) Redis-L2 cross-replica rate-limit accuracy still open (carried 2 phases); (3) no body-size cap on relayed Hydra response; (4) ory/hydra#3723 external-aud operator caveat. Recommended next phase: agent-gateway-exposure-operability -- shared cross-replica rate-limit/Redis-L2 FIRST (gates horizontal exposure), then delegate-mode observability + optional re-stamp, operator guardrails (https-only hydra URLs, body cap, startup posture refusing to expose /oauth/* without introspect_auth + rate-limiting), E2E exposure smoke tests vs a real Ory stack. Stay authorization-first; federate any JWKS IdM, never an IdP; LLM-ops bundle stays out of scope.
